synthesis.summary: Partner (Rhino) raised a P1 believing FT/SOC had agreed to monitor a Pragmatic platform release; FT determined internally no such commitment existed. Partner sync confirmed complete 14 Jul 18:53 UTC, no platform issue found on FT side. Giancarlo closed the Intercom ticket with the partner at 06:11 UTC 15 Jul (CSAT sent). No new activity since; incident.io status still reads 'Waiting on Partner', unchanged.

synthesis.summary: Shuffle/Data-Studio CF-WAF false-positive blocking dashboards for internal + partner users (also hit Supabets). Patrik Potocki personally wrote the fix (WAF rule PR #15, merged ~10:58 CEST) and drafted the partner-facing explanation himself at 11:11 CEST. As of 12:17 CEST (66 min later) it still hadn't gone out - Patrik had to chase Joachim directly ('did you communicate?'), CC'ing Andrea Azzopardi, and told him not to just copy-paste the draft verbatim. Joachim acknowledged being 'a bit busy' and said he'd send it.

synthesis.summary: Maxima ClickHouse-writer PVC bump (1380Gi->1600Gi) didn't actually hold - the resize request never reached AWS, likely because two rapid back-to-back PVC edits left the EBS-CSI resizer's retry state wedged. Lead handed from Gérard E. Pelayo to Joachim Farrugia, who dove into root cause and merged in a related incident (INC-14080). Genuine, active technical investigation now underway; incident.io still 'Fixing'.
key_events: 01:29:42Z created, auto-declined 15min later, recommendation unactioned
05:31:44Z Joachim joins, monitoring
06:26-06:27Z reopened
06:32-06:33Z PVC upgraded 1380Gi->1600Gi
06:49:43Z remaining alert resolved
08:40 CEST 15 Jul: incident.io status still 'Fixing', not closed ~2h since resolution
11:46 CEST Joachim Farrugia takes over as Lead from Gérard E. Pelayo; INC-14080 merged in
11:47 CEST Joachim identifies the PVC resize never issued ControllerExpandVolume to AWS - resizer likely wedged after the rapid double edit
proposals: Increase/persist ClickHouse PVC size - applied live; persistence in IaC/config not yet confirmed
open_breaches: C10/D15: incident.io still 'Fixing'; the earlier PVC bump didn't actually take effect and a new lead is now actively re-diagnosing root cause
synthesis.why: C10: the previously-applied PVC fix didn't actually take (resize request never reached AWS, EBS-CSI resizer likely wedged); new lead Joachim is actively diagnosing root cause now, incident.io still shows 'Fixing'.

synthesis.summary: G2 disputed a Welcome-Offer bonus for player 556 ($42.79 credited vs $69.99 owed). Lead passed from Gérard to Matteo Rapisarda, who fully reconstructed the timeline: G2's own casino events arrived 8-29 min late that evening (lag reached ~80min by 23:00 on 10 Jul), so the bonus fired correctly on the data available at the time - not a formula bug. Matteo replied to the partner with the full root cause and moved the incident to Waiting on Partner. He also flagged, unprompted, that other players who hit NGR-based milestones during that lag window could have similar discrepancies - not yet investigated.

synthesis.summary: SMS distributor service has not processed queued messages for all Coinbar brands since before 30 Apr - over 1M messages backlogged. Root cause (external SMS provider outage) identified immediately, but 2.5+ months later there is zero forward progress. Confirmed nothing has moved since 29 May besides more alerts auto-merging in: a further Hizlicasino alert merged in at 15 Jul 10:24 CEST (no consumers, 1376 messages), all machine-generated with no human commentary.
key_events: 30 Apr: incident created, 1M+ message backlog
25-29 May: Andrea Azzopardi repeatedly asks for an explanation, no answer for 4 days

15 Jul 10:24-11:24 CEST: another Hizlicasino alert (1654 msgs, no consumers) merged in; 50+ alerts auto-resolved twice more - still zero human commentary since 29 May
proposals: Escalate to SMS provider - done repeatedly, no concrete response ever
Gustav Tjäder asked to advise next course of action (29 May) - never answered
open_breaches: A4: chronic multi-month issue never escalated to a decisive fix or vendor-management track - now 47 days of pure automated churn
D12/D15: the one substantive question raised (29 May) never answered or tracked - Lead rotated to at least a 6th person, new alerts keep merging in unattended
synthesis.why: A4/D12: 2.5+ months of pure automated alert churn with zero human decision-making since 29 May; incident.io and Slack both confirm the only activity since is another auto-merged Hizlicasino alert (15 Jul) - no human has posted in 47 days.
synthesis.suggested_action: Force a decision: escalate to account/vendor management for a binding SMS-provider ETA or a migration decision, and assign a named owner who must respond rather than letting the lead rotate unattended.
